questions.prompt: Triangle ABC has vertex A at (-3, 2). Under the translation T(x, y) = (x + 5, y - 4), what are the coordinates of A'?
questions.explanation: Apply the rule directly: A' = (-3 + 5, 2 - 4) = (2, -2). Translations slide every point by the same vector, preserving distance and angle measure (an isometry).

questions.prompt: Point P(4, -3) is rotated 90° counterclockwise about the origin. What are the coordinates of P'?
questions.explanation: A 90° counterclockwise rotation about the origin maps (x, y) to (-y, x). So P(4, -3) maps to P'(-(-3), 4) = (3, 4). Rotations are isometries that preserve orientation.

questions.prompt: Point Q(5, -2) is reflected over the line y = x. What are the coordinates of Q'?
questions.explanation: Reflection over the line y = x swaps the coordinates: (x, y) maps to (y, x). So Q(5, -2) maps to Q'(-2, 5). Reflections reverse orientation but preserve distance.

questions.prompt: Triangle ABC has vertex B(6, -9). Under a dilation centered at the origin with scale factor 2/3, what are the coordinates of B'?
questions.explanation: Multiply each coordinate by the scale factor: B' = (6 · 2/3, -9 · 2/3) = (4, -6). Dilations preserve angle measure and produce similar (not congruent) figures.

questions.prompt: Which sequence of transformations is NOT an isometry?
choices: A translation followed by a rotation
A reflection followed by a translation
A rotation followed by a dilation with scale factor 3
A reflection followed by a rotation
questions.correctText: A rotation followed by a dilation with scale factor 3
questions.explanation: An isometry preserves distance. Translations, rotations, and reflections are all isometries. A dilation with scale factor 3 changes distances, so any composition that includes it (when k ≠ ±1) is not an isometry.

questions.prompt: In triangles ABC and DEF, AB ≅ DE, BC ≅ EF, and m∠B = m∠E. Which postulate proves △ABC ≅ △DEF?
questions.explanation: Two pairs of sides (AB ≅ DE, BC ≅ EF) and the included angle (∠B ≅ ∠E) are congruent. Side-Angle-Side (SAS) requires the angle to be between the two sides, which is exactly the case here.

questions.prompt: In right triangles ABC and DEF, ∠C and ∠F are right angles, AB ≅ DE (hypotenuses), and AC ≅ DF. Once △ABC ≅ △DEF is proven, which statement follows by CPCTC?
questions.explanation: The triangles are congruent by HL (Hypotenuse-Leg). CPCTC (Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles are Congruent) then guarantees BC ≅ EF, the remaining pair of corresponding legs.

questions.prompt: In parallelogram WXYZ, diagonal WY is drawn. Which statement must be true?
questions.explanation: In a parallelogram, opposite sides are parallel, so WY is a transversal creating two pairs of congruent alternate interior angles. With the shared side WY, the triangles are congruent by ASA. Diagonals only bisect vertex angles in a rhombus and are only perpendicular in a rhombus or square.

questions.prompt: A quadrilateral has diagonals that are congruent and bisect each other but are NOT perpendicular. The quadrilateral must be a:
questions.explanation: Congruent diagonals that bisect each other characterize a rectangle. If they were also perpendicular, it would be a square. Rhombus diagonals are perpendicular but not necessarily congruent; trapezoid diagonals do not generally bisect each other.

questions.prompt: In △RST, RS ≅ RT and m∠R = 40°. What is m∠S?
questions.explanation: By the Isosceles Triangle Theorem, the base angles ∠S and ∠T are congruent. Since the angle sum is 180°, m∠S + m∠T + 40° = 180°, giving 2·m∠S = 140°, so m∠S = 70°.

questions.prompt: Which set of properties is sufficient to prove that a quadrilateral on the coordinate plane is a parallelogram?
choices: Both pairs of opposite sides have equal slopes
One pair of opposite sides is parallel
questions.correctText: Both pairs of opposite sides have equal slopes
questions.explanation: Equal slopes show that both pairs of opposite sides are parallel — the definition of a parallelogram. Four congruent sides describes a rhombus (a special parallelogram, but more than needed); congruent diagonals describe a rectangle; one pair of parallel sides only guarantees a trapezoid.

questions.prompt: In △ABC and △DEF, m∠A = m∠D = 55° and m∠B = m∠E = 75°. Which statement is true?
choices: The triangles are congruent by ASA
The triangles are similar by AA
The triangles are similar only if AB = DE
questions.correctText: The triangles are similar by AA
questions.explanation: Two pairs of congruent angles guarantee similarity by the Angle-Angle (AA) postulate. The third angle in each triangle must equal 180° - 55° - 75° = 50°, but no side information is given, so they are similar (not necessarily congruent).

questions.prompt: In right △ABC with the right angle at C, AB = 17 and BC = 8. What is sin(A)?
questions.explanation: Sine equals opposite over hypotenuse. The side opposite ∠A is BC = 8, and the hypotenuse is AB = 17. So sin(A) = 8/17. (The remaining leg AC = 15, since 8-15-17 is a Pythagorean triple.)

questions.prompt: From a point on level ground 40 feet from the base of a flagpole, the angle of elevation to the top of the pole is 35°. To the nearest foot, how tall is the flagpole?
questions.explanation: Using tan(35°) = height / 40, the height = 40 · tan(35°) ≈ 40 · 0.7002 ≈ 28.0 feet. Tangent relates the opposite side (height) to the adjacent side (ground distance).

questions.prompt: In △ABC, a = 7, b = 9, and m∠C = 60°. Which expression gives the length of side c?
questions.explanation: Law of Cosines: c² = a² + b² - 2ab·cos(C) = 49 + 81 - 2(7)(9)(1/2) = 49 + 81 - 63. Since cos(60°) = 1/2, the 2ab·cos(C) term simplifies to 63.

questions.prompt: In circle O, central angle AOB intercepts arc AB. If m(arc AB) = 80°, what is the measure of an inscribed angle ACB that intercepts the same arc?
questions.explanation: The Inscribed Angle Theorem states that an inscribed angle is half the measure of its intercepted arc. So m∠ACB = 80°/2 = 40°. The central angle, by contrast, equals the arc measure (80°).

questions.prompt: Line t is tangent to circle O at point P. Which statement must be true?
choices: Line t passes through center O
Line t is perpendicular to radius OP at point P
Line t is parallel to radius OP
Line t is a chord of the circle
questions.correctText: Line t is perpendicular to radius OP at point P
questions.explanation: A tangent line to a circle is perpendicular to the radius drawn to the point of tangency. This is a foundational circle theorem used in many Regents problems.

questions.prompt: Two chords intersect inside a circle, forming an angle whose intercepted arcs measure 70° and 110°. What is the measure of the angle?
questions.explanation: The measure of an angle formed by two chords intersecting inside a circle equals half the sum of the intercepted arcs: (70° + 110°)/2 = 180°/2 = 90°.

questions.prompt: What is the equation of a circle with center (-2, 5) and radius 4?
questions.explanation: The standard form is (x - h)² + (y - k)² = r². With center (h, k) = (-2, 5) and radius r = 4, the equation is (x - (-2))² + (y - 5)² = 4² = 16, or (x + 2)² + (y - 5)² = 16.

questions.prompt: What is the midpoint of the segment connecting A(-1, 7) and B(9, -3)?
Line l has the equation y = (2/3)x + 5. Which equation represents a line perpendicular to l that passes through the point (4, -1)?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular slopes are negative reciprocals: the perpendicular slope is -3/2. Using point-slope form: y - (-1) = -(3/2)(x - 4), which simplifies to y = -(3/2)x + 6 - 1 = -(3/2)x + 5.

questions.prompt: Point P partitions segment AB from A(2, 1) to B(12, 11) in the ratio 2:3. What are the coordinates of P?
questions.explanation: To partition AB in ratio 2:3, P is 2/5 of the way from A to B. P = (2 + (2/5)(12 - 2), 1 + (2/5)(11 - 1)) = (2 + 4, 1 + 4) = (6, 5).

questions.prompt: A quadrilateral has vertices A(0, 0), B(6, 0), C(8, 4), and D(2, 4). Which classification BEST describes this quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: Slope of AB = 0, slope of DC = 0 (parallel). Slope of AD = 4/2 = 2, slope of BC = 4/2 = 2 (parallel). So it is a parallelogram. AB = 6 but AD = sqrt(4+16) = sqrt(20), and the sides AB and AD are not perpendicular (slope 0 and slope 2), so it is not a rectangle.

questions.prompt: A right cylinder has radius 5 cm and height 12 cm. What is its volume, in cubic centimeters?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = π·r²·h = π·5²·12 = π·25·12 = 300π cubic centimeters.

questions.topic: Measurement: Volume of Cone vs. Cylinder
questions.prompt: A cone and a cylinder share the same radius and the same height. The volume of the cone is what fraction of the volume of the cylinder?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = π·r²·h; volume of a cone = (1/3)·π·r²·h. So the cone is exactly one-third the volume of the matching cylinder. This relationship also holds between pyramids and prisms with congruent bases and equal heights.

questions.prompt: Cavalieri's Principle states that two solids have equal volumes if:
choices: They have the same surface area
They have congruent bases and equal heights
Every plane parallel to a fixed plane cuts cross-sections of equal area through both solids, and the heights are equal
They have the same number of vertices and edges
questions.correctText: Every plane parallel to a fixed plane cuts cross-sections of equal area through both solids, and the heights are equal
questions.explanation: Cavalieri's Principle: if two solids of equal height have cross-sectional areas equal at every level, their volumes are equal. This is why an oblique cylinder has the same volume as a right cylinder with the same base and height.

questions.prompt: When constructing the perpendicular bisector of segment AB with a compass and straightedge, you draw two arcs of the same radius from A and from B. What must this radius satisfy?
questions.explanation: The arcs must intersect on both sides of segment AB. If the radius were (1/2)·AB or less, the arcs would meet only on AB itself (or not at all). Any radius greater than (1/2)·AB produces two intersection points, and the line through them is the perpendicular bisector.

questions.prompt: To construct an equilateral triangle on segment AB with compass and straightedge, you set the compass to length AB, draw an arc from A, then draw an arc of the same radius from B, and label the intersection point C. Why is △ABC equilateral?
choices: Because the arcs intersect at the midpoint of AB
Because each arc has radius AB, so CA = CB = AB
Because the perpendicular bisector of AB passes through C
questions.correctText: Because each arc has radius AB, so CA = CB = AB
questions.explanation: Point C lies on both arcs of radius AB, so CA = AB and CB = AB. All three sides are equal in length, making △ABC equilateral by definition.

questions.prompt: To construct a regular hexagon inscribed in a circle, you can step the compass around the circle. What setting should the compass have?
choices: Half the radius of the circle
Equal to the radius of the circle
Equal to the diameter of the circle
Equal to the circumference divided by 6
questions.correctText: Equal to the radius of the circle
questions.explanation: In a regular hexagon inscribed in a circle, each side equals the radius. So stepping the compass set to the radius around the circle produces exactly six equally spaced points — the vertices of the regular hexagon.

questions.prompt: A right circular cylinder is sliced by a plane that passes through both circular bases and is parallel to the axis. What shape is the resulting cross-section?
questions.explanation: A plane parallel to the axis of a right circular cylinder, cutting through both bases, produces a rectangular cross-section. A plane perpendicular to the axis would produce a circle; an oblique plane would produce an ellipse.

questions.prompt: A grain silo is modeled as a cylinder of radius 6 ft and height 20 ft topped by a hemisphere of radius 6 ft. What is the total volume of the silo, in cubic feet?
questions.explanation: Cylinder volume = π·r²·h = π·36·20 = 720π. Hemisphere volume = (1/2)·(4/3)·π·r³ = (2/3)·π·216 = 144π. Total = 720π + 144π = 864π cubic feet.
